2606.30246.title: Clarus: Coordinating Autonomous Research Agents toward Web-Scale Scientific Collaboration
2606.30246.paper_summary: Existing autonomous research agents can support parts of the research process, but most systems still treat research as either an isolated assistant task or a closed workflow. Therefore, autonomous science needs a collaboration infrastructure that coordinates projects, agents, and digital and physical resources. We identify this as a shift from code-centered execution loops to research-oriented collaboration processes, where questions, evidence, participants, and resources must be coordinated under uncertainty. In this framing, an agent may be an AI system, a human researcher, a team, a laboratory, or an organization-backed participant. To this end, we present Clarus, a collaboration infrastructure for coordinating autonomous research agents toward web-scale scientific collaboration. Clarus reformulates research as an open, auditable, attributable, and resource-aware multi-phase collaboration process. It defines a minimal project-agent-resource object model and organizes scientific collaboration through four layers including Research Application, Digital Collaboration, Physical Substrate, and Physical World. Core modules are implemented as pluggable mechanisms, allowing Clarus to adapt to task risk, collaboration structure, and resource constraints. Through a controlled paper-generation case study, we show that Clarus can organize a research goal into a traceable, reviewable, attributable, and accumulative collaboration network across phases, tasks, and participants. Together, the object model, collaboration protocol, trust mechanisms, and prototype validation provide an initial foundation for open research networks. Clarus is now available at clarus.holosai.io.
2606.30246.paper_summary_zh: 現有的自主研究代理可以支持研究過程的某些部分，但大多數系統仍然將研究視為孤立的助手任務或封閉的工作流程。因此，自主科學需要一個協作基礎設施，以協調項目、代理以及數位和實體資源。我們將這視為從以代碼為中心的執行循環轉向以研究為導向的協作過程，其中問題、證據、參與者和資源必須在不確定性下進行協調。在這種框架下，代理可以是AI系統、人類研究者、團隊、實驗室或組織支持的參與者。為此，我們提出了Clarus，一個協作基礎設施，用於協調自主研究代理以實現網絡規模的科學協作。Clarus將研究重新定義為一個開放的、可審計的、可歸因的和資源感知的多階段協作過程。它定義了一個最小的項目-代理-資源對象模型，並通過包括研究應用、數位協作、實體基礎和物理世界在內的四個層次來組織科學協作。核心模塊被實現為可插拔的機制，允許Clarus根據任務風險、協作結構和資源限制進行調整。通過一個受控的論文生成案例研究，我們展示了Clarus如何將研究目標組織成一個可追溯、可審查、可歸因和累積的協作網絡，跨越階段、任務和參與者。總體而言，對象模型、協作協議、信任機制和原型驗證為開放研究網絡提供了初步基礎。Clarus現在可以在clarus.holosai.io上使用。
2606.30246.author: Zihan Guo et.al.
2606.30246.authors: Zihan Guo, Zeyi Chen, Zhiyu Chen, Zicai Cui, Shuai Shao, Bo Huang, Zhi Han, Yuanyi Song, Yuan Yuan, Chenxi Zeng, Xiaohang Nie, Zhengxi Yu, Hanwen Zhu, Junwei Liao, Ming Zhou, Yang Li, Yuanjian Zhou, Weinan Zhang
